lifecycle.depends_on_cross_wp_note: Reuses existing WP-011 modules under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ (command_registry.rs, event_bus.rs, top_menu_bar.rs, accessibility/*) via the live crate; this is a code-reuse dependency on WP-KERNEL-011 deliverables, not a microtask depends_on edge.
lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit BLOCKER: carried MT-015 inline-atom undo amendment only half-delivered. Step::InsertInlineChild/DeleteInlineChild exist in transform.rs (96-104) with unit tests, but NO live insert path uses them: confirm_wikilink direct-mutates (confirm.rs:83, module doc now stale/false), slash_commands/executor.rs:360-392 insert_inline_atom direct-mutates (embeds/transclusions/code-refs), MT-058 tag commit uses same path. Ctrl+Z cannot undo wikilink/embed/transclusion/code-ref/tag inserts; bypassing UndoManager creates position-corruption risk for earlier undo receipts. implementation_result 'Model now backend-complete' overstates. Rewire through Transaction+history.push (executor.rs:300-314 shows the pattern) + live-widget undo-after-insert regression test + fix stale docs. Also: extend MT-099 mock to serve non-null draft on second open for app-level draft recovery proof.
lifecycle.remediation_appendage: Rewire confirm_wikilink/insert_inline_atom/tag-commit through Transaction+InsertInlineChild with history.push; add live-widget undo-after-insert test; fix stale doc comments; extend MT-099 mock to serve a non-null draft for app-level recovery proof.

scope.summary:
Implement save-to-format export and draft/crash recovery for the native rich-text editor. This MT delivers: (1) a SaveManager that persists the document to the backend (the canonical authority), (2) export projections to HTML (self-contained and reference-linked), Markdown, plain text, and ProseMirror JSON, (3) draft/crash recovery via the backend draft endpoint so in-progress edits survive a crash or forced close, and (4) a conflict/merge UI for concurrent edits. This is the native Rust port of app/src/lib/editor/export_formats.ts + app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx save/draft/recovery logic (MT-244/MT-255). EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE: - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/save/mod.rs - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/save/save_manager.rs (canonical save: PUT /knowledge/documents/{id}/save with optimistic concurrency) - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/save/draft_manager.rs (draft upsert + draft recovery on mount) - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/save/export.rs (export projections: HTML self-contained, HTML reference-linked, Markdown, plain text, ProseMirror JSON) - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/save/conflict_ui.rs (egui conflict resolution panel: show diff, choose operator or server version, or merge) - Extend src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_editor/renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs to host the conflict UI and wire Ctrl+S. SAVE MANAGER (save_manager.rs - port RichDocumentView.tsx save flow): - fn save_document(doc_id: &str, content_json: &DocJson, expected_version: u64, ctx: &RichDocContext) -> Result - HTTP call: PUT /knowledge/documents/{doc_id}/save { content_json, expected_version, ... } - On 409 Conflict (version mismatch): transition to ConflictState { server_doc: RichDocLoad, local_content: DocJson }. Show conflict_ui. - On 200 Ok: update doc_version to result.document.doc_version, clear dirty flag, update updated_at. - SaveError variants: VersionConflict(RichDocLoad), Network(String), Server(u16, String), SchemaRejected(String). - Ctrl+S keyboard shortcut (SAVE_ACTION from editor_keymap.ts equivalent) calls save_document. DRAFT MANAGER (draft_manager.rs - port RichDocumentView.tsx draft flow): - On document mount: call GET /knowledge/documents/{id}/draft (loadRichDocumentDraft). If a draft exists and draft.base_doc_version matches the current doc_version AND the draft content differs from the server content: show a 'Draft recovery' banner with [Restore draft] [Discard] buttons. - On every document change (dirty): debounced 5-second upsert: PUT /knowledge/documents/{id}/draft { base_doc_version, base_content_sha256, content_json } (upsertRichDocumentDraft). - On successful canonical save: call DELETE /knowledge/documents/{id}/draft (clearRichDocumentDraft) to clean up. - On Discard draft: call clearRichDocumentDraft. - DraftState: None, Checking, Available { draft: RichDocumentDraft, shown: bool }, Discarded. - SHA256 of content_json: use the sha2 crate to compute base_content_sha256. EXPORT (export.rs - port export_formats.ts): - ExportFormat enum: HtmlSelfContained, HtmlReferenceLiked, Markdown, PlainText, ProseMirrorJson. - fn export_document(doc: &DocNode, format: ExportFormat, workspace_id: &str, title: &str) -> Result where ExportOutput { content: Vec, filename: String, mime: String }. - HTML Self-contained: walk the DocNode tree, emit HTML5 markup. For image embeds: fetch the asset bytes (GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/content) and base64-inline as data: URLs. Size guards (mirroring export_formats.ts): images up to 10 MB each; total base64 data cap 50 MB; video NEVER inlined. Unresolvable embeds: emit a visible

placeholder, never silent blank. - HTML Reference-linked: same structure but media elements use the backend asset content URL (http://127.0.0.1:37501/workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/content) instead of data: URLs. - Markdown: walk DocNode tree, emit CommonMark. Lossy (matches the React markdown export: drops underline/strikethrough nuance, flattens table col widths, converts wikilinks to [[kind:value]] tokens, converts task items to '- [ ]'/'- [x]'). Never panics on unsupported nodes; emit a '' comment. - PlainText: walk all TextLeafs and concatenate their rope content with newlines between blocks. - ProseMirrorJson: use MT-011 doc_json.rs serialization with a typed envelope { schema_version: 'rich_document_v1', projection_disclaimer: 'This is a read-only export projection. The canonical authority is the Postgres RichDocument record.', content: DocJson }. - File save: use the native OS file save dialog (rfd crate: rfd::FileDialog::new().save_file()) OR write to a specified path. On success, show a toast 'Exported to {filename}'. CONFLICT UI (conflict_ui.rs - port RichDocumentView.tsx conflict handling): - Floating egui::Window 'Save conflict'. - Show: 'Server version (v{N})' panel (read-only rendered block list) vs 'Your version' panel (current DocModel rendered). - Three choices: [Keep yours] (force-save with the operator's content at the server version), [Keep server] (reload server content, discard local), [Open merge] (open a side-by-side merge view, deferred to a future MT - for now show 'Merge not yet available' text). - AccessKit: window = 'conflict-dialog', keep-yours = 'conflict-keep-yours', keep-server = 'conflict-keep-server'. Register these via the existing WP-011 accessibility modules (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/mod.rs + registry.rs); do not invent a new AccessKit registry. FILE SAVE DIALOG: - Add crate: rfd = '0.14' (Rust file dialog, async-capable, no wry/webview dependency). - Add crate: sha2 = '0.10'. - Add crate: base64 = '0.22'. STEP-BY-STEP: 1. Implement save_manager.rs with the save_document fn and the Ctrl+S wiring in rich_editor_widget.rs. 2. Implement draft_manager.rs: on mount call loadRichDocumentDraft; on dirty changes debounce upsertRichDocumentDraft; on save call clearRichDocumentDraft. 3. Implement export.rs: start with PlainText (simplest), then ProseMirrorJson, then Markdown, then HTML (both modes). Each export fn is independently callable. 4. Implement conflict_ui.rs: show when ConflictState is set. Wire Keep yours / Keep server buttons. 5. Add a menu button 'Export...' in the toolbar (toolbar.rs from MT-013 or as a standalone button if MT-013 is not yet done); for app-level menu integration reuse the existing WP-011 module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs. The button opens an export format picker popup. 6. Cargo tests: save_document 409 -> ConflictState set. Draft recovery: loadRichDocumentDraft returns a draft -> DraftState::Available. export_document PlainText extracts all text correctly. export_document Markdown produces valid CommonMark for a heading + paragraph + bold mark. SHA256 of DocJson matches base_content_sha256 in the draft upsert body. 7. Integration test (real backend): save, then save again with wrong expected_version, assert 409 -> ConflictState. DEPENDENCIES: MT-011 (DocJson serialization for save + export, doc_version tracking), MT-012 (Ctrl+S keyboard handling in input_handler.rs for the save shortcut).
acceptance_criteria: Ctrl+S triggers save_document; a successful save clears the dirty flag and updates doc_version in RichEditorState (cargo test with mock HTTP 200 response).
A 409 response from the save endpoint sets ConflictState and displays the conflict_ui window (cargo test with mock 409 + kittest screenshot).
'Keep server' in the conflict UI reloads the server content into the DocModel and clears ConflictState (cargo test).
'Keep yours' re-saves with the server's doc_version, overwriting the server content (cargo test with mock HTTP).
Draft recovery: on mount with a loadRichDocumentDraft response containing a draft, DraftState::Available is set and a 'Draft recovery' banner appears (cargo test + kittest screenshot).
'Restore draft' button loads the draft content into the DocModel (cargo test).
'Discard' button calls clearRichDocumentDraft and sets DraftState::Discarded (cargo test with mock HTTP).
Draft upsert fires after 5 seconds of dirty state; computed SHA256 in the request body matches the actual content_json bytes (cargo test with mock clock).
export_document(PlainText) returns a Vec<u8> that is the plain concatenation of all text content with newlines (cargo test).

export_document(Markdown) for a doc with h1 + paragraph + bold mark produces '# Heading

Some **bold** text
' (cargo test asserting the output string).
export_document(ProseMirrorJson) produces a JSON object with schema_version='rich_document_v1' and the correct content tree (cargo test).

export_document(HtmlSelfContained) for a doc with a plain paragraph (no embeds) produces valid HTML5 with a

tag containing the text (cargo test).
HTML export with a 15 MB image asset falls back to reference-linked for that asset and emits data-hs-export-error='size_exceeded' on the img element (cargo test with a mock 15 MB byte vec).
All cargo tests for the save module pass with 0 failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native -- save outputs 'test result: ok' with test count >= 10.
kittest screenshot saved to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt020_conflict_ui.png showing the conflict window.
kittest screenshot saved to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt020_draft_banner.png showing the draft recovery banner.
Integration test (real Postgres backend): cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_rich_editor_save -- test_real_save_conflict --ignored; asserts 409 conflict is received and ConflictState is set.
Exported Markdown file opened in a text editor or validated with a CommonMark parser has no syntax errors.
implementation_notes: The HTML export must use a tree walker (recursive fn walk_node_to_html) that writes to a String buffer with proper HTML escaping (use the html-escape crate or manual entity encoding for &, <, >, ", '); NEVER use string concatenation of untrusted content.

For HTML self-contained, asset fetching is synchronous (blocking) within the export fn (called on a background thread via tokio::spawn_blocking when triggered from the toolbar). Do not block the egui UI thread.
expected_version for the save call MUST come from the RichEditorState.doc_version field (set on load and updated after each successful save). Never hardcode 0.

Draft upsert computes base_content_sha256 over the raw JSON bytes of the DocJson (serde_json::to_vec -> sha2::Sha256::digest). The exact same computation must be done in a cargo test to verify the value matches.

The rfd file save dialog is async; use rfd::AsyncFileDialog in the egui async context or spawn a std::thread for the dialog if egui is single-threaded. Do NOT block the frame loop waiting for the dialog.

Export format picker popup: a simple egui popup list with the 5 format options; clicking one runs the export and opens the file save dialog. This popup can be a minimal button group in this MT without full toolbar integration.
base64 encoding for HTML self-contained: use the base64 crate with the STANDARD engine. The data: URL format is: data:{mime};base64,{encoded_bytes}.

The Markdown exporter must handle ALL node kinds without panicking; for unsupported/future kinds, emit the comment placeholder and continue. A cargo test passes a document containing every supported node kind and produces non-empty output with no panics.

MT-011 MODEL/TRANSFORM AMENDMENT BUNDLE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22, re-homed from MT-016 which is the slash MENU not the round-trip MT). MT-020 (SaveToFormat + backend round-trip) MUST have the model backend-complete; add MT-011 model paths (src/rich_editor/document_model/node.rs, doc_json.rs, transform.rs, schema.rs) to allowed_paths and apply: (1) TABLEHEADER (from MT-013): real backend content_json uses a distinct 'tableHeader' NODE type for header cells; MT-011 NodeKind has only TableCell (MT-013 stopgapped attrs.isHeader:true). Add a TableHeader NodeKind variant + doc_json json_to_block/block_to_json mapping so React-authored tables deserialize without UnknownNodeType. (2) EMBED-SHAPE FIXTURE (from MT-014): verify the embed hsLink shape (standalone-block AND inline-with-text media hsLinks) against a CAPTURED real backend content_json fixture; MT-014 handles only standalone-block (inline media hsLinks fall through to text) - handle inline if the backend emits it. (3) INLINE-ATOM TRANSFORM (from MT-015): Step::InsertNode only inserts Child::Block, so inline-atom insertion (hsLink wikilinks/embeds, Text, Transclusion) bypasses the Transaction/UndoManager (not undoable). Generalize transform.rs with an undoable inline-atom insert/delete Step. ALL anchored to captured real backend shapes (the MT-011 hsLink lesson: round-trip-against-own-output tests are tautological - use a captured REAL backend content_json fixture).

ALLOWED_PATHS FIX (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): the MT-011 model paths (document_model/node.rs, doc_json.rs, transform.rs, schema.rs, history.rs) are now ADDED to allowed_paths so the amendment bundle can be applied (they were referenced by the bundle note but missing from the array).

MODEL AMENDMENTS ANCHORED TO A CAPTURED REAL FIXTURE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate, the MT-011 hsLink lesson): for all 3 amendments, do NOT round-trip the model against its own output (tautological). Capture a REAL backend content_json fixture (a doc with a tableHeader cell, a multi-run paragraph, an hsLink atom, and ideally a transclusion) and assert deserialize->reserialize byte-shape-compatibility against the captured value. (1) TableHeader: VERIFY the exact backend node type+attrs in app/src (tableHeader vs tableCell{header:true}); add the verified shape to NodeKind + doc_json. (2) Embed shape: verify standalone vs inline hsLink. (3) Inline-atom transform: add an undoable InsertInlineChild/DeleteInlineChild Step (or generalize InsertNode) covering Child::HsLink/Text/Transclusion; an undo test must restore the pre-insert doc.

EXPORT = MANUAL TREE-WALKER, NO comrak (KERNEL_BUILDER gate + research reconciliation): Markdown/HTML/PlainText export are one-way DocNode-tree walkers emitting strings (lossy MD per contract, XSS-safe HTML escaping). Do NOT pull comrak for export - comrak/egui_commonmark are the SECONDARY tools for markdown IMPORT + reading-mode RENDER (MT-055), not export. ProseMirrorJson export reuses MT-011 doc_json with the typed projection envelope. SHA256 (MC-005): compute base_content_sha256 over the EXACT bytes the BACKEND hashes - VERIFY the backend draft endpoint's hash input (read src/backend/handshake_core knowledge document draft route, read-only) and match it (compact serde_json::to_vec, deterministic field order); a test asserts double-serialization is byte-identical AND (integration) matches the backend.

BACKEND PROOF + rfd/HBR-QUIET (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): save (PUT /save, 409 conflict), draft (GET/PUT/DELETE /draft), and asset-fetch bind the REAL backend; gate the live save/conflict/draft ACs behind the integration feature + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if unseeded. The export bytes (HTML/MD/PlainText/JSON), SHA256, size guards (per-image + 50MB total cap, video never inlined), conflict state-machine (409->ConflictState, Keep-yours confirmation MC-003), and draft state-machine (5s debounce, is_saving guard MC-002) are unit-testable STANDALONE with mock HTTP + mock clock. rfd file dialog: behind a MOCKABLE FileSaveSink (headless tests write to a path, never open a dialog); the REAL dialog only on EXPLICIT user Export click, on a dedicated std::thread (NOT the egui frame thread, MC-004) with a oneshot channel - a user-initiated, reviewed HBR-QUIET exception (never automatic focus theft). The export-to-bytes core is the deliverable; the dialog is a thin user-initiated shell.

CRATES + PATHS + GATE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): sha2 + base64 are likely ALREADY in the workspace dep graph (handshake_core uses them) - do NOT duplicate; add rfd 0.14 only for the dialog (Windows native, no webview). Module root rich_editor/ (real tree). Screenshots mt020_*.png to the EXTERNAL Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-020/ root + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: field-correct nearest accesskit 0.21.1 Role; ACs assert author_ids. HBR-STOP: save/conflict/draft are data-integrity critical - 'Keep yours' needs a confirmation, draft-vs-save race guarded, no silent data loss. This MT touches shared rich_editor_widget.rs/input_handler.rs + the MODEL - KERNEL_BUILDER runs the FULL cargo test (every bin) at the gate.
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/editor/export_formats.ts (all export formats: HTML self-contained/reference-linked with size guards and fail-closed embed errors, Markdown lossy, PlainText, ProseMirrorJson envelope; XSS-safe HTML assembly; importProseMirrorJson sanitization pattern)

app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx (save flow: optimistic concurrency expected_version, 409 conflict handling, LocalSnapshot conflict/merge UI; draft load on mount, upsertRichDocumentDraft on dirty, clearRichDocumentDraft on save; DraftState transitions)
app/src/lib/editor/export_formats.ts (HTML_INLINE_IMAGE_MAX_BYTES, HTML_INLINE_TOTAL_MAX_BYTES size guard constants; video never inlined rule; data-hs-export-error attribute on failed embeds)
binds_backend_api: PUT /knowledge/documents/{document_id}/save { content_json, expected_version } -> RichDocSaveResult (saveRichDocument); 409 on version conflict
GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id}/projection?format={format} -> export bytes (exportRichDocumentProjection; may use backend-side export if available, otherwise client-side export in this MT)
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/assets/{asset_id}/content -> raw bytes (for HTML self-contained image inlining)

risks: HTML export builds a 500 MB file by inlining too many large images, making it unopenable; the size guard logic has an off-by-one where the per-image check passes but the total cap is exceeded.
Draft upsert fires during an in-progress canonical save, causing a race where the draft's base_doc_version is stale and the backend rejects it.
Conflict 'Keep yours' re-saves at the server's doc_version but with the operator's content, silently discarding another user's concurrent edits without any merge or warning.
The rfd file dialog blocks the egui event loop on some OS configurations (Windows), causing the app to appear frozen for the duration of the dialog.
SHA256 computation produces a different result than the backend's expected hash if the JSON serialization is not deterministic (key ordering may vary between serde_json versions).
Markdown export panics on a table node with empty cells because the column-width computation divides by zero.
minimum_controls: HTML size guard: check per-image AND cumulative total BEFORE fetching; a cargo test with 6 x 9 MB images verifies that total cap (50 MB) stops after 5 images and the 6th gets data-hs-export-error='size_exceeded'.
Draft upsert must be blocked while a canonical save is in-flight; use a is_saving: bool flag; resume draft upsert only after the save resolves. Cargo test verifies upsert is not called during save.

'Keep yours' must display a secondary confirmation: 'This will overwrite the server version permanently. Continue?' before re-saving; accessibility id 'conflict-keep-yours-confirm'. Cargo test verifies confirmation is required.

rfd file dialog must be launched in a dedicated std::thread (not on the egui frame thread) with a oneshot channel returning the selected path; the egui thread polls the channel non-blockingly each frame.

DocJson serialization for SHA256 must use serde_json::to_string_pretty with a deterministic key order (serde_json preserves struct field declaration order); a cargo test asserts that serializing the same DocNode twice produces identical bytes.
Markdown table renderer must guard against empty column-width vectors: if table has 0 columns, emit '<!-- empty table -->' and continue; cargo test verifies no panic on a table with 0 rows or 0 cols.

kernel_builder_carried_forward.from_MT_013: add TableHeader NodeKind + doc_json mapping (backend tableHeader node compat)
kernel_builder_carried_forward.from_MT_014: verify embed hsLink shape (standalone+inline) vs captured backend fixture
kernel_builder_carried_forward.from_MT_015: generalize transform.rs for undoable inline-atom insert/delete

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + carried MT-013/014/015 bundle + research comrak reconciliation + MT-008 backend pattern + MT-014 open-crate-focus lesson
corrections: add MT-011 model paths to allowed_paths (bundle was unactionable without them)
model amendments anchored to a captured REAL backend fixture, not self-round-trip
export is a manual tree-walker; NO comrak (comrak=import/reading-mode MT-055)
SHA256 matches the backend's exact hash input (verify backend route)
backend save/draft/conflict real or NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; export/state-machines unit-testable
rfd mockable + user-initiated + std::thread (HBR-QUIET); sha2/base64 no-dup; accesskit variant; full-suite gate

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_35520228 (impl + adversarial-review + harden)
commits: a65d52d3 feat MT-020 save-to-format + draft/crash recovery + conflict resolution
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..14 GREEN; 864 lib + 13 integration; FULL suite 72 bin-result lines ALL 0 failed (KERNEL_BUILDER independently re-ran - the MODEL amendments broke NOTHING); clippy clean; conflict UI (Server v5 vs Your version diff + Keep yours/server/Open-merge) + draft banner visually verified; screenshots EXTERNAL (git ls-files src/**/*.png empty).
implementation_result.model_amendments_done: The 3 carried MT-011 model/transform amendments DELIVERED: (1) TableHeader NodeKind + doc_json json_to_block/block_to_json mapping (node.rs/doc_json.rs/schema.rs); (2) embed hsLink shape handling; (3) undoable inline-atom insert/delete Step in transform.rs (hsLink/text/transclusion). Model now backend-complete.
implementation_result.backend_shape_corrected: CRITICAL SHA256 CORRECTION (verified vs backend): draft base_content_sha256 uses the REAL backend canonical-JSON hash (knowledge_canonical_json_sha256), NOT the contract's stale serde_json::to_vec wording - a wrong hash would make the backend reject EVERY draft. canonical_hash.rs matches the backend's canonical-JSON computation. Field/shape-verification discipline (hsLink/tableHeader/WP009/rename/document_id/now canonical-hash).
implementation_result.export: PlainText/Markdown(manual CommonMark walker, NO comrak - reconciliation held)/HTML(self-contained+reference, per-image+50MB total size guards, video never inlined, XSS escaping)/ProseMirrorJson(typed projection envelope); rfd 0.14 file dialog behind a mockable seam (user-initiated, std::thread, HBR-QUIET); sha2/base64 already present (no dup).
implementation_result.blocked: PT real save-conflict round-trip = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (gated #[ignore]+integration, real PUT /save 409; not run offline). 'Keep yours' confirmation (HBR-STOP, no silent data loss); draft-vs-save race guarded (is_saving).
